name: United Kingdom (Royal Fleet Auxiliary Ensign)

colors.symbolism: the red details of the Union canton
the white details of the Union canton
the upright anchor of the Royal Fleet Auxiliary

description: A Blue Ensign defaced in the fly with an upright gold anchor; the Union Flag is in the canton.
history: A distinct ensign for the Royal Fleet Auxiliary — a Blue Ensign with a plain vertical gold anchor — was approved by Queen Elizabeth II in 1968 and introduced from 16 June 1969. The upright anchor distinguishes it from the earlier government auxiliary ensign, which bore a horizontal anchor.

features.symbolism: the Blue Ensign field of British government service
the Union Flag, marking the ensign as British

features.role: upright anchor of the Royal Fleet Auxiliary
